verses.text: Guard your steps in God's house; be quick to hear and slow to speak, for 'God is in heaven, and thou upon earth: therefore let thy words be few.' Reverence governs worship. We come to listen and submit, not to chatter rashly before the Most High.

Guard your steps in God's house; be quick to hear and slow to speak, for 'God is in heaven, and thou upon earth: therefore let thy words be few.' Reverence governs worship. We come to listen and submit, not to chatter rashly before the Most High.

Guard your steps in God's house; be quick to hear and slow to speak, for 'God is in heaven, and thou upon earth: therefore let thy words be few.' Reverence governs worship. We come to listen and submit, not to chatter rashly before the Most High.

When you vow to God, pay it, for He has no pleasure in fools; 'better is it that thou shouldest not vow, than... not pay.' Keep your word to God. Promises lightly made and broken provoke Him; 'fear thou God.'

When you vow to God, pay it, for He has no pleasure in fools; 'better is it that thou shouldest not vow, than... not pay.' Keep your word to God. Promises lightly made and broken provoke Him; 'fear thou God.'
Marvel not at the oppression of the poor by officials, for higher ones watch above them, and over all is God. Injustice has its overseers. The highest of all sees and will reckon with every wrong.
Marvel not at the oppression of the poor by officials, for higher ones watch above them, and over all is God. Injustice has its overseers. The highest of all sees and will reckon with every wrong.

'He that loveth silver shall not be satisfied with silver'; more goods bring more mouths to feed, while the labourer sleeps sweetly and the rich man's abundance robs him of rest. Money never fills the heart. Contentment, not wealth, gives peace.

'He that loveth silver shall not be satisfied with silver'; more goods bring more mouths to feed, while the labourer sleeps sweetly and the rich man's abundance robs him of rest. Money never fills the heart. Contentment, not wealth, gives peace.

'He that loveth silver shall not be satisfied with silver'; more goods bring more mouths to feed, while the labourer sleeps sweetly and the rich man's abundance robs him of rest. Money never fills the heart. Contentment, not wealth, gives peace.
Riches hoarded to the owner's hurt perish, and 'as he came... naked shall he return,' carrying nothing of his labour. We take nothing out of the world. The grave strips away all that greed piled up.
Riches hoarded to the owner's hurt perish, and 'as he came... naked shall he return,' carrying nothing of his labour. We take nothing out of the world. The grave strips away all that greed piled up.
Riches hoarded to the owner's hurt perish, and 'as he came... naked shall he return,' carrying nothing of his labour. We take nothing out of the world. The grave strips away all that greed piled up.
This is a sore evil: a man labours for the wind, spending his days in darkness, sorrow, and wrath. Toil for mere gain is empty. A life given to getting ends in shadow and grief.
This is a sore evil: a man labours for the wind, spending his days in darkness, sorrow, and wrath. Toil for mere gain is empty. A life given to getting ends in shadow and grief.

Yet it is good to eat, drink, and enjoy one's labour, for this is God's gift; the man whom God fills with joy 'shall not much remember the days of his life.' Gladness is grace. To enjoy God's daily gifts is to be carried lightly through life.
